title: Septulina glauca
taxonomy: Septulina glauca is classified as a species of plant in the family Rhamnaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the buckthorn or dogwood family. This family comprises around 600 species, many of which are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Septulina glauca is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions. The plant is an evergreen shrub or small tree that grows up to 10 meters tall. Its classification is further broken down to the genus level, with Septulina being a part of the Rhamnus genus.
